canonical_definition: The construct of graph understanding is defined in two distinct ways within the provided literature, primarily differing on the modality of the graph representation. One framing, sometimes termed "Graph comprehension," defines it as the ability to interpret "structural and relational information encoded in a visual graph representation" ("GraphVis: Boosting LLMs with Visual Knowledge Graph Integration"). This approach focuses on a model's ability to understand the "features of a visual graph" and is targeted through methods like "curriculum fine-tuning." A second framing defines the construct as the "latent ability to interpret and reason over graph-structured information represented with textual attributes" ("G-Retriever: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Textual Graph Understanding and Question Answering"). This version targets "real-world textual graphs" and is enhanced via techniques such as "soft prompting." In both conceptualizations, the ability is operationalized in the context of question answering tasks.
papers: G-Retriever: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Textual Graph Understanding and Question Answering
GITA: Graph to Visual and Textual Integration for Vision-Language Graph Reasoning
GraphVis: Boosting LLMs with Visual Knowledge Graph Integration

definitions.text: The ability to understand and interpret the structural and relational information encoded in a visual graph representation.
definitions.paper: GraphVis: Boosting LLMs with Visual Knowledge Graph Integration

definitions.text: The latent ability to interpret and reason over graph-structured information represented with textual attributes.
definitions.paper: G-Retriever: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Textual Graph Understanding and Question Answering
